---
title: "Text Analysis GenCom: Generating Code and Analysis"
output: html_document
date: "2026-07-06"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

This is a note pad for the data preparation and initial analysis of the General Comments Paper (Network and Diskurs Analysis).

# Transform Documents
The first step is to transform and merge the data. I exported the text data from two MAXQDA files that contain the network coding and all the available texts. MAXQDA only allows exports in Excel, which -- however -- only allows up to ca. 32.000 characters per line. Although the MAXQDA export orinigally stores the text in several lines, the text analysis requires text in one line to generate a comprehensive data set. 
Thus, the transformation needs to transform the text first into CSV and than generate the data set.



## Prepare

```         
install.packages("readxl")
install.packages("ggplot2")

setwd("D:/Research/Web of Human Rights/GenComs/text")

```

## Transforming Excel into CSV
Excel files are stored in one separate folder. CSV files go into a new folder for all texts.

```
library(readxl)

input_dir  <- "all_xlsx"
output_dir <- "all"

files <- list.files(input_dir, pattern = "\\.xlsx$", full.names = TRUE)

for (f in files) {
  df <- tryCatch({
    read_excel(f, col_names = FALSE)
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("FEHLER beim Einlesen von ", basename(f), ": ", e$message)
    NULL
  })
  
  if (is.null(df)) next  # Datei überspringen, aber Loop läuft weiter
  
  out_name <- paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(f)), ".csv")
  out_path <- file.path(output_dir, out_name)
  
  tryCatch({
    write.csv(df, out_path, row.names = FALSE, fileEncoding = "UTF-8")
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("FEHLER beim Schreiben von ", basename(f), ": ", e$message)
  })
}

```

## Batching over all docs
This needs to run over all GenCom Docs now in CSV format.

```         
library(dplyr)

files <- list.files("all", pattern = "\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
docs_list <- lapply(files, read.csv, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, encoding = "UTF-8")
names(docs_list) <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(files))

collapse_doc <- function(df, text_col = 2) {
  paste(df[[text_col]], collapse = "\n\n")
}

collapsed_all <- sapply(docs_list, collapse_doc)


dataset_text <- data.frame(
  doc_id = names(collapsed_all),
  text   = collapsed_all,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
```

## Exporting Data set just with text

```         
#write text in external file
writeLines(dataset$text, "datatext.txt")

#export dataset
write.csv2(dataset, "dataset_text.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```


## Merge with network data 

```
setwd("D:/Research/Web of Human Rights/GenComs/wd")
library(dplyr)

# Data set 2 with info
data_gc <- read.csv("data_gc.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

## Check
# Values in doc_id without match in data_gc$id 
only_in_text <- setdiff(dataset_text$doc_id, data_gc$id)
only_in_text
length(only_in_text)

# Values in id without match in dataset_text$doc_id
only_in_gc <- setdiff(data_gc$id, dataset_text$doc_id)
only_in_gc
length(only_in_gc)

# Merge
dataset_text <- dataset_text %>% rename(id = doc_id)

dataset_gc <- dataset_text %>%
  inner_join(data_gc, by = "id")

# Check
nrow(merged)
nrow(dataset_text)
nrow(data_gc)

```


## Exporting Data set this version

```         
#export dataset
write.csv2(dataset_gc, "dataset_gc.csv", row.names = FALSE)


View(dataset_gc)                    # View in RStudio
str(dataset_gc$text[87])             # Single text
writeLines(dataset_gc$text[87])      # Read doc

```

## Further code


```



```





## Emerging Problems



# To Do
 (Keep titles) (?)
 
 
# Analysis

## Set-up

´´´
setwd("D:/Research/Web of Human Rights/GenComs/wd")
library(ggplot2)

´´´

## Plotting trend in words


´´´
dataset_gc |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = words)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess",
  method.args = list(degree = 2, span = 0.75)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number Words",
    title = "Words in General Comments and Recommendations over Time"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

´´´




clustering
sementic distribution
readability
supervised classification


- how is the discursive authority organized, is there a textual dimension to explain the network position

RQ: Does the text explain the network position? 


- specific framing of a new HR interpretation: VaW Gen Com on shifting human rights interpretation, --> look for examples that represent the textual/discursive linkages that you expect
non-technical references --> classification or extraction , name entry recognition task
- What are text level co-variance that make the text (in terms of theory network) central?

- What about GenComs jointly produced by Committees?

+ contructing the Data set
++ Overview Data
- doc_id, text, indegree, outdegree, betweenness, 
- 
++ Dyadic Network Data
- basic doc_id dyadic data

- Identify examples to grasp what these textual representation might actualyl look like.

+ Analysis: supervised extraction, undersupervised extraction








## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```

```

# ############################################################################
# Appendix
This includes old ideas and residual code.

## First idea Read texts

```         
# Read data
UN2017GenComCAT4 <- read_excel("UN2017GenComCAT4.xlsx")

# Collapse into a single string, paragraphs separated by double newline
doc_text <- paste(UN2017GenComCAT4[[2]], collapse = "\n\n")

# If you want it as a one-row tibble preserving the structure:
UN2017GenComCAT4_collapsed <- tibble(
  doc_id = 1,
  committee = "Committee against Torture",
  text = paste(UN2017GenComCAT4 [[2]], collapse = "\n\n")
)

#if you need to batch this across documents than I would do soemthing like
this
collapse_doc <- function(df, text_col = 2) {
  paste(df[[text_col]], collapse = "\n\n")
}
```
## Old bathcing code
```
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)

files <- list.files("all", pattern = "\\.xlsx$", full.names = TRUE)
docs_list <- lapply(files, read_excel)
names(docs_list) <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(files))

collapse_doc <- function(df, text_col = 2) {
  paste(df[[text_col]], collapse = "\n\n")
}

collapsed_all <- sapply(docs_list, collapse_doc)


dataset <- data.frame(
  doc_id = names(collapsed_all),
  text   = collapsed_all,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

#Check
lengths <- sapply(docs_list, function(df) nchar(df[[2]]))
lengths  # oder: 
which(lengths > 32000)


```


\*\*Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.



library(openxlsx)
setwd("D:/Research/Web of Human Rights/GenComs/wd")

## CSV einlesen
df <- read.csv("data_gc.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

## Neues Workbook erstellen
wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "Daten")

## Daten schreiben
writeData(wb, "Daten", df)

## Spaltenbreite automatisch anpassen
setColWidths(wb, "Daten", cols = 1:ncol(df), widths = "auto")

## Kopfzeile formatieren (fett, Hintergrundfarbe)
headerStyle <- createStyle(textDecoration = "bold", fgFill = "#D9E1F2", 
                            halign = "center", border = "Bottom")
addStyle(wb, "Daten", headerStyle, rows = 1, cols = 1:ncol(df))

## Filter auf Kopfzeile aktivieren
freezePane(wb, "Daten", firstRow = TRUE)

## Speichern
saveWorkbook(wb, "dataset_formatiert.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)


### Another attempt to get good text data extracted
#### test
library(readxl)

files <- list.files("all_xlsx", pattern = "\\.xlsx$", full.names = TRUE)
docs_list <- lapply(files, read_excel)
names(docs_list) <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(files))

## Zeichenlängen aller Zeilen in der Textspalte prüfen
check_lengths <- lapply(docs_list, function(df) nchar(as.character(df[[2]])))

## Wie viele Zeilen pro Dokument sind exakt 32767 Zeichen lang?
sapply(check_lengths, function(x) sum(x == 32767, na.rm = TRUE))

## Zur Sicherheit auch mal die Maximalwerte pro Dokument anschauen
sapply(check_lengths, max, na.rm = TRUE)



#### run


files <- list.files("all_xlsx", pattern = "\\.xlsx$", full.names = TRUE)
docs_list <- lapply(files, read.csv, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, encoding = "UTF-8")
names(docs_list) <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(files))

collapse_doc <- function(df, text_col = 2, threshold = 32760) {
  txt <- as.character(df[[text_col]])
  txt[is.na(txt)] <- ""
  
  result <- character(0)
  buffer <- ""
  
  for (i in seq_along(txt)) {
    buffer <- paste0(buffer, txt[i])
    # Wenn die Zeile NICHT am Limit abgebrochen wurde, ist der Textblock fertig
    if (nchar(txt[i]) < threshold) {
      result <- c(result, buffer)
      buffer <- ""
    }
    # Sonst: nächste Zeile direkt anhängen (KEIN \n\n, da mitten im Text getrennt)
  }
  if (nchar(buffer) > 0) result <- c(result, buffer)
  
  paste(result, collapse = "\n\n")
}



#### doc suche
## Beispiel: erstes Dokument, zeig mir die letzten 100 Zeichen jeder Zeile
df <- docs_list[[80]]
sapply(df[[2]], function(x) substr(as.character(x), nchar(as.character(x)) - 99, nchar(as.character(x))))

df <- read_excel("all_xlsx/UN1997GenRecCEDAW23.xlsx", col_types = "text")

df_alt <- read_excel("all_xlsx/UN1997GenRecCEDAW23.xlsx")
sum(is.na(df_alt[[2]]))
nchar(paste(df_alt[[2]], collapse = ""))

## Nachher (mit col_types = "text")
df_neu <- read_excel("all_xlsx/UN1997GenRecCEDAW23.xlsx", col_types = "text")
sum(is.na(df_neu[[2]]))
nchar(paste(df_neu[[2]], collapse = ""))



##

df_alt <- read_excel("all_xlsx/UN1997GenRecCEDAW23.xlsx")
cat("Alt - NAs:", sum(is.na(df_alt[[2]])), "\n")
cat("Alt - Zeichen gesamt:", nchar(paste(df_alt[[2]], collapse = "")), "\n")

df_neu <- read_excel("all_xlsx/UN1997GenRecCEDAW23.xlsx", col_types = "text")
cat("Neu - NAs:", sum(is.na(df_neu[[2]])), "\n")
cat("Neu - Zeichen gesamt:", nchar(paste(df_neu[[2]], collapse = "")), "\n")


collapse_doc <- function(df, text_col = 2) {
  paste(df[[text_col]], collapse = "\n\n")
}

doc_lengths <- sapply(docs_list, function(df) nchar(collapse_doc(df)))
doc_lengths

## Wie viele Dokumente liegen über dem Excel-Limit?
sum(doc_lengths > 32767)
which(doc_lengths > 32767)



## Verify

## Datensatz neu einlesen (nicht über Excel, sondern direkt in R)
check <- read.csv2("dataset_all.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

## Zeichenlängen vergleichen mit dem Original-dataset
all.equal(nchar(dataset$text), nchar(check$text))

## Speziell die vorher identifizierten "langen" Dokumente prüfen
long_docs <- which(doc_lengths > 32767)
nchar(dataset$text[long_docs])       # Original
nchar(check$text[long_docs])         # Neu eingelesen aus CSV
